ref: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
text: Di saha ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Dinŋɔ wulisiri, ni, zaaligu karimbasɔ ŋɔn labgi Arizana naam yɛla nyaandɔla ŋmanni la yiridaana ŋɔn kpeeri u neema duu ni n‑yiisiri bumpaala na, ka yiisi bunkura na m‑paasi.”
translated: On the day you arrived, they told you, saying: “Listen, wise one, they spoke of the story of the land of Arizona that occurred among the people, where there was fighting, because you took from my elder brother’s house and you took my wife.”

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.
translated: Communities should work hard on their farmlands so that we thrive; but if people are idle on their farmlands, then we suffer, therefore it is said that we depend on our farmlands.

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Bee bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi suŋŋa? Ya bu nya? - ninvugseeba ban yeeri bunveela n kur bɛ nadima yiya ni.”;
translated: Bee, what is this matter that you are saying you have done? What do the people say? Do you know? — Those who listen will explain the truth, and we will clearly understand.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: U daa bu kpalim Nazarɛti tiŋŋa, amaa u daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ Kapirinyum tiŋŋa ni, tinseaa kan zɛ mɔ'ari gbiriŋŋu ni, Zɛbulɔn ni Naftaali tingbana ziiya.
translated: He says that the prayer is from Nazareth, or he says the word is from Capernaum, and the people are the children of God there, Zebulun and Naphtali are united.

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Ba daa wukki ba zugri saazugu, amaa ba daa bu nya sɔa; kulla Yisa kɔkɔ n daa zɛya.
translated: Those who do not listen to the wise, or do not do good deeds; all Yisa's mouth says the truth.

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: “Raama ka kukɔri maa yi na, Kunkuma ni fabligu pam n nyɛ di. Reekyɛl n kumni u biisi zugu Halli ka sɔ ka ni n‑nin toaai n‑goaai u; Dama u biisi maa zaa na ka ni.”
translated: Raama and the story will be told now; Here are the words with which I hold these. Reekyɛl, I call you to come and Halli says that you are my given and taken; Then when you come, you will go now.

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Nyin daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni!”
translated: Our supplicating lord is here! Indeed, we hear that you answer our supplications and give so that you satisfy our needs, and may you also always be with joy and truly answer us!

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ka u bumburiseesi mun daa lu kuga ziiya ka bɛni tanni daa bu zoaai, ka a daa tɔ bula yoomyooma dama tanni maa daa pɔsigi a.
translated: Listen carefully to what I say about the people's well-being, know the time of their existence, and here every day also obey what I say.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Yɛlimaŋni ka n siri yɛtti ya, ni, u nin ti zaŋŋi u bunseelli kam fukumsi n‑niŋi lala daabri nuu ni.
translated: Those who say that I hear what they bring, and you know that you do well with my word in my heart, truly your way is.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa zinni bɛni ka gɔsiri u ni.
translated: The house and those people who are looking for them.

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Amaa m boori la ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba duniya wa ni.” Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa,
translated: You and I come here now let's listen: "People tell me that there is chaos in the world, indeed, but those who invoke the Lord Biiya, I need protection from my enemies here in the world." So now you and I must listen carefully.

ref: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
text: Di saha ka Naawunni niriba nin nya'ara, hoo wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn nya'ari seem maa, m‑bɛ ba Ba naam maa ni. Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma!”
translated: On this day that Naawunni recalls my youth, when those youths were playing together here, I did not know the world's way then. Now truly I see my suffering, these troubles!

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: n‑yɛtti, ni, “Ya kyɛli ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri zaa, dama Arizana naam bɛ la ya saani.”
translated: And he said, "His house, his village, and his field are there, but the people of Arizana live in their homes."

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Di daa niŋiya ka Yisa daa yi Nazarɛti, tinseaa kan bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni la, ka daa kyɛ na nti kpɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari maa ni n‑su koom Gyɔni nuu ni.
translated: We say that Jesus has gone to Nazareth, all who are in Galilee in that land, and we declare that the great God has come to us, sending His Spirit to us.

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Ka niriba pam daa mari ba baaridima n‑kyɛnni u saani na, gbarisi ni wɔbsi n daa bɛ ni, ni zoomma ni tubkpira dima, ni banŋɔ taabu pam. Ba da zaŋŋi ba m‑basi Yisa nɔba ziiya, ka u daa tibi ba zaa.
translated: Because we keep hearing talk about this matter that the chiefs say they are hiding it, even though the people are living there, in the village without development, with various problems. They don't provide us with the basic necessities, so they act like that.
